factors.name: Mega-cap tech sentiment
factors.summary: Tesla +8% on self-driving update; AeroVironment +19% on earnings beat; Strategy +12% on capital plan. Single-name momentum in QQQ heavyweights supporting broader NDX.

factors.name: Fed independence affirmation
factors.summary: Supreme Court ruled in favor of Fed independence vs. Trump removal attempts. Reduces near-term regulatory uncertainty for tech mega-caps and reduces tail risk of political interference in monetary policy.

factors.name: AI capex and robotics narrative
factors.summary: Waymo-DoorDash autonomous delivery expansion; Rocket Lab acquiring Iridium for $8B; Salesforce on AI buying spree (3 deals in June). Hyperscaler AI investment theme intact.

factors.name: Treasury yields steady
factors.summary: US 10Y yields flat at 4.37%; no surprise inflation print or Fed repricing event. Long-duration growth multiples stable, no tailwind or headwind to valuations.

factors.name: Dollar strength (USD breakout)
factors.summary: DXY broke 40-year trading range; USD/JPY at 161.97 (40-year high). Strong dollar headwind to multinational tech earnings and international revenue drag on NQ components.

factors.name: Real yields (TIPS)
factors.summary: No explicit TIPS data released; gold down $72 to ~$4,015 suggests real yields stable-to-slightly-higher. Modest headwind to long-duration tech, but not acute.

factors.name: Geopolitical risk easing
factors.summary: US-Iran interim deal; talks scheduled for Qatar Tuesday. Oil prices rising but tension-driven volatility subsiding. Risk-on sentiment supporting equities, reduces tail VIX expansion risk.

factors.name: China macro weakness
factors.summary: Bloomberg: China stocks lagging most since 2001 despite AI advances; PBoC new overnight facility dovish signal at 1.25%. China growth concerns weigh on commodity, EEM, and semi-cyclical demand.

factors.name: Japan data miss
factors.summary: May industrial production +0.5% m/m vs. +1.1% expected; -1.7% y/y. Weak yen (161.97) not translating to export strength. Regional growth concerns.

factors.name: Regulatory tailwind (Trump court wins)
factors.summary: Supreme Court allows Trump to fire FTC/SEC commissioners at will, overturning Humphrey's Executor precedent. Reduces regulatory friction for big tech (antitrust concern lower near-term); crypto regulation also eases with executive discretion.

factors.name: Crypto correlation volatility
factors.summary: Bitcoin at $60K with put-call ratio at 1-year high (bear positioning); ETF outflows persist. BTC-NQ linkage risk-on/off, but not decisively breakout-bullish. Noise around Strategy MSTR volatility.

factors.name: Earnings visibility near-term
factors.summary: No major mega-cap earnings releases in imminent 24-72h news window. Forward guidance and Q3 outlook remain key unknowns until earnings season; current move driven by sentiment and positioning, not fundamentals.

screening.narrative: NAS100 rallying +1.72% on the back of positive mega-cap tech sentiment (Tesla, AeroVironment, Strategy), Supreme Court affirmation of Fed independence, and geopolitical risk de-escalation (US-Iran interim deal). The move is in Phase 2 — underway but not yet extended — supported by AI narrative staying intact (Salesforce, Rocket Lab M&A) and regulatory tail risks diminishing. However, strong USD (DXY breakout, USD/JPY 40-year high) creates a meaningful headwind for international NQ revenue, and China macro weakness (Bloomberg report on stock underperformance) raises cyclical growth concerns. Japan data miss and dovish PBoC signals suggest regional demand softening.
catalysts_ahead: US NFP (jobs report) — likely Friday, could reprice Fed rate expectations
RBA minutes Tuesday (overnight Asian session) — hints at tightening bias; AUD-sensitive, cross-flows into NQ
China official PMI (Tuesday, Asian hours) — manufacturing health critical for semiconductor demand
ECB Forum on Central Banking (already started Monday, continues) — Lagarde signaling dovish hold; EUR weakness = USD strength = NQ headwind
FOMC minutes or any Fed speaker commentary — rate path clarity
Mega-cap tech earnings calendar — forward guidance on cloud capex, AI demand, margin outlook
screening.risk_warning: Move is already +1.72% in 24h and +0.16% in 4h; if this rally extends another 1%+ without retest, late-stage positioning risk increases. Sharp USD strength could surprise downside on multinational tech earnings revisions. China growth concerns (PMI Tuesday) could spark rotation out of growth/mega-cap tech into value/defensives. FOMC or inflation surprise could reprice Treasury yields upward, crushing long-duration multiples. Geopolitical risk remains live (Iran talks fragile); any escalation resets risk appetite sharply lower.
screening.entry_guidance: Move is in Phase 2 and moderately extended; avoid chasing at current levels. Optimal entry on pullback to 29,700–29,750 (support), or wait for Fed/inflation clarity before adding. If break above 30,000, next resistance 30,100–30,200, but insufficient confluence to warrant new longs here.

factors.name: China PMI beat (Manufacturing 50.3 vs 50.1 exp)
factors.summary: China factory PMI exceeded expectations at 50.3, driven by AI-linked export demand. This supports risk-on sentiment and benefits NDX mega-caps with heavy China exposure (AAPL, MSFT, TSMC supply chain).

factors.name: Tech sector rebound narrative
factors.summary: Bloomberg reports 'US Tech Rebounds' with Asia trade showing strong inflows into tech on AI buildout optimism. Mega-cap FAANG names positioned to benefit from sustained capex cycles.

factors.name: USD strength / DXY above 101.30
factors.summary: USD Index strengthens above 101.00 on Fed rate hike bets, which pressures duration-sensitive NDX constituents and international revenue streams. Inverse correlation risk for growth tech.

factors.name: Yen intervention risk (USD/JPY 162.40, 40-year low)
factors.summary: Japanese yen hits weakest level since 1986; Japanese officials signal readiness for intervention at any time. Sudden BOJ/MOF action could trigger sharp yen rally, dollar weakness, and risk-asset pullback.

factors.name: Real yields / inflation repricing
factors.summary: Gold drops below $4,000 on inflation concerns and higher real rate expectations. Long-duration tech (NVDA, TSLA, MSFT) sensitive to real yield compression; current backdrop suggests sticky inflation fears limiting multiple expansion.

factors.name: Oil price stabilization (WTI ~$70, down from $70+)
factors.summary: Oil retreats as Iran-US talks progress and Hormuz reopens; Morgan Stanley warns of glut risk. Lower energy costs ease margin pressure for tech but reduce geopolitical risk premium.

factors.name: Crypto weakness (Bitcoin sub-$60k, Ether/Solana slide)
factors.summary: CoinDesk reports crypto pullback on Strategy's bitcoin sales plan and yen weakness. NAS100 has shown BTC correlation on risk-off days; weakness here signals underlying risk-asset caution.

factors.name: 24h price action: +1.72% move already in place
factors.summary: NDX already rallied 1.72% in 24h, suggesting move is in early PHASE_2. Further upside may face profit-taking unless confirmed by strong earnings or macro catalyst.

factors.name: No major earnings or Fed event imminent (next 48h)
factors.summary: Calendar clear near-term; next high-impact catalysts likely CPI, NFP, or FOMC in next 1-2 weeks. Lack of immediate catalyst caps conviction.

factors.name: Antitrust / regulatory overhang (Apple India, Amazon Australia)
factors.summary: Minor but ongoing regulatory pressure on mega-caps (AAPL, AMZN). Supreme Court DMA clarity eases some concern but does not eliminate medium-term risk.

screening.narrative: Nasdaq 100 has rallied 1.72% in 24h on China PMI beat and tech sentiment rebound driven by AI narrative. However, rally faces headwinds from strengthening USD (DXY 101.30), real yield pressure (gold below $4000), and yen intervention risk (USD/JPY at 40-year high near 162.40). Crypto weakness and crypto-stock correlation suggest underlying risk appetite is fragile. Move is in PHASE_2; further upside momentum depends on either a major positive catalyst (strong mega-cap earnings, dovish Fed surprise) or a pullback to retest support, which would offer a cleaner entry.
catalysts_ahead: US Jobs Report (NFP) — likely early July; inflation-sensitive, impacts Fed rate expectations
ECB rate decision on July inflation surprise — sources flag ECB could move lower if June CPI is weak
BOJ board member Sato speech (0800 GMT today) — potential hawkish surprise could strengthen yen, trigger intervention, and reverse risk-on rally
Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon earnings — typically move NDX 1–3% each; awaited for cloud capex guidance
US-Iran talks in Doha (ongoing today) — geopolitical resolution or escalation could reprice oil and risk sentiment
FOMC decision (mid-July) — major repricing event for long-duration growth multiples
screening.risk_warning: Yen intervention would likely trigger sharp risk-off reversal and NAS100 pullback 1–2%. Real yield spike (10Y yield >4.5%) or inflation surprise could compress NDX multiples. Earnings miss from mega-cap (NVDA, MSFT, AAPL) or weak hyperscaler capex guidance would invalidate AI rally narrative and sink index. Strong USD continuation (DXY >102) also headwinds for international-heavy tech revenue.
screening.entry_guidance: Move already up 1.72% in 24h — avoid chasing above 29900. Wait for pullback to 29500–29650 support or confirm break above 30000 resistance on strong macro catalyst (earnings beat, dovish Fed signal) before scaling long. High risk of profit-taking or yen intervention shock before next significant catalyst.
